text: The Public Bath is fed by a deep spring and heated by a furnace that the attendants stoke before dawn. Tiled in cracked blue and white, the main pool steams gently. A wooden bench runs the perimeter, and a small side room holds a cold plunge for those who need waking up rather than calming down.
Steam drifts across the water. The only sounds are the drip of a tap and the distant murmur of two old men who appear to have been here since yesterday.
